title: U.S. Dollar Exchange Rate
primary_source: wdi (PA.NUS.FCRF) + IMF IFS historical + Bahmani-Oskooee (2005) + TGJU
alt_sources: navasan.tech (free-market USD, daily OHLC 2019-2026 + latest); brsapi.ir (USD + USDT/IRT latest)
notes: Add as an additional corroborating source line on the PARALLEL variant -- do NOT replace TGJU. 2,524 daily closes from navasan ohlcSearch (item=usd_sell) spanning 2019-02-16 to 2026-07-14, giving the project a genuinely INDEPENDENT second daily source over a window where only TGJU covered it. The two providers are unrelated and agree closely: median navasan/TGJU ratio 1.0002 across 2,006 overlapping days, 97.7% of days within +/-5%. That mutual agreement is itself strong validation of BOTH series. Also extends 3 days past the chart's current last point (TGJU 2026-07-11, 1,782,000 rial/USD) through 2026-07-14 (navasan 1,839,900 rial/USD; brsapi USD 1,839,900 rial/USD, same-day agreement). Earliest date navasan serves is 2019-02-15 -- its archive genuinely starts there (a start=1380/2001 request returned rows beginning only at that date), so this cannot backfill the pre-2019 era. Scale verified empirically, not assumed: navasan's ohlcSearch values are plain toman (x10 -> rial), cross-checked against this project's own independent TGJU series across the full overlap -- median navasan/TGJU ratio 1.0002 over 2,006 overlapping days. A 10x unit error would have shown as a ratio of 0.1 or 10; it did not. NOTE navasan's /latest/ endpoint uses a DIFFERENT scale for the coin fields (thousands of toman, x10,000) despite identical field names -- see data/processed/iran_api_market_series/README.md before reusing any raw navasan number. Suggested citations to add: 'navasan.tech (api.navasan.tech, free-market USD rate, corroborating source, accessed 2026-07-14)' and 'brsapi.ir (api.brsapi.ir/Market/Gold_Currency.php, corroborating source, accessed 2026-07-14)'.

title: U.S. Dollar Exchange Rate
primary_source: wdi (PA.NUS.FCRF) + IMF IFS historical + Bahmani-Oskooee (2005) + TGJU
alt_sources: Central Bank of Iran (cbi.ir/exrates/rates_fa.aspx), official/NIMA-weighted rate
notes: FLAGGING FOR COORDINATOR: the chart's OFFICIAL variant is frozen at 42,000 rial/USD for every year 2019-2023 (source wdi_ifs_official) and stops there -- WDI has stopped tracking the actual drift of Iran's official rate. CBI's OWN live page (cbi.ir/exrates/rates_fa.aspx, USD row, labeled the NIMA-system volume-weighted rate) shows the official rate itself now at 1,359,712 rial/USD as of 2026-07-14 -- roughly 32x the stale 42,000 figure. The chart's OFFICIAL line therefore currently understates the official rate by more than an order of magnitude for recent years, which also distorts the PREMIUM_PCT variant computed from it. This does NOT conflict with the project's 'use the parallel rate for 1979-present' policy (docs/bookkeeping.md): that rule governs which rate feeds computed real/USD conversions elsewhere, whereas THIS chart deliberately plots OFFICIAL and PARALLEL side by side to show the gap, so an accurate current OFFICIAL line is precisely what it needs. REAL FIND: the CBI site was directly reachable (plain curl and WebFetch both succeeded) -- it was NOT geo-blocked as the brief anticipated, so a fuller 2024-2026 official-rate series is very likely buildable from CBI's own historical exrates pages (incl. Wayback snapshots, the same technique already used for the 2004-2010 CBI verification). This agent pulled only a single current-day anchor, per the brief's 'secondary priority, don't spend long there' guidance. Recommend a dedicated follow-up pass. Suggested citation: 'Central Bank of Iran (cbi.ir/exrates), official/NIMA-weighted rate, accessed 2026-07-14.'

title: Bahar Azadi Gold Coin Price, Rial, Daily (2013-07-22 to 2026-07-11)
alt_sources: navasan.tech (bahar, daily OHLC 2019-2026 + latest); brsapi.ir (IR_COIN_BAHAR latest)
notes: Add as an additional corroborating source line -- do NOT replace TGJU. 2,562 daily closes from navasan ohlcSearch (item=bahar), 2019-06-09 to 2026-07-14. This chart previously had a SINGLE source (TGJU, alt_sources empty); it now has independent corroboration across 1,961 overlapping days, median navasan/TGJU ratio 0.9955, 90.8% of days within +/-5% (the wider dispersion vs. the USD series is expected -- coin prices carry a volatile dealer premium over melt value and differ more between quoting venues than FX does). Also extends 3 days past the chart's last point (TGJU 2026-07-11, 1,721,800,000 rial) through 2026-07-14 (navasan 1,750,000,000; brsapi 1,750,200,000 -- the two APIs agree with each other to within 0.01% on the same day). navasan's archive starts 2019-06-09, so this cannot backfill the chart's 2013-2019 era. Scale verified empirically, not assumed: navasan's ohlcSearch values are plain toman (x10 -> rial), cross-checked against this project's own independent TGJU series across the full overlap -- median navasan/TGJU ratio 0.9955 over 1,961 overlapping days. A 10x unit error would have shown as a ratio of 0.1 or 10; it did not. NOTE navasan's /latest/ endpoint uses a DIFFERENT scale for the coin fields (thousands of toman, x10,000) despite identical field names -- see data/processed/iran_api_market_series/README.md before reusing any raw navasan number. Suggested citations: 'navasan.tech (api.navasan.tech, corroborating source, accessed 2026-07-14)' and 'brsapi.ir (api.brsapi.ir/Market/Gold_Currency.php, corroborating source, accessed 2026-07-14)'.
